medical-doctors.desc_snippet: Medical Doctors in Germany increased to 4.98 per 1000 people in 2021 from 4.90 per 1000 people in 2020. Medical Doctors in Germany averaged 3.93 per 1000 people from 1991 until 2021, reaching an all time high of 4.98 per 1000 people in 2021 and a record low of 3.04 per 1000 people in 1991.  source:

military-expenditure.desc_snippet: Military Expenditure in Germany increased to 113585.90 USD Million in 2025 from 86148.30 USD Million in 2024. Military Expenditure in Germany averaged 27551.93 USD Million from 1953 until 2025, reaching an all time high of 113585.90 USD Million in 2025 and a record low of 1381.80 USD Million in 1953

minimum-wages.desc_snippet: Minimum Wages in Germany increased to 13.90 EUR/Hour in 2026 from 12.82 EUR/Hour in 2025. Minimum Wages in Germany averaged 10.38 EUR/Hour from 2015 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 13.90 EUR/Hour in 2026 and a record low of 8.50 EUR/Hour in 2016.  source:  Federal Statistical Office

mining-production.desc_snippet: Mining production in Germany decreased 4.80 percent in March of 2026 over the same month in the previous year. Mining Production in Germany averaged -3.64 percent from 1992 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 46.60 percent in March of 2007 and a record low of -30.40 percent in February of 2010.

money-supply-m1.desc_snippet: Money Supply M1 in Germany increased to 2906 EUR Billion in February from 2905 EUR Billion in January of 2026. Money Supply M1 in Germany averaged 864.87 EUR Billion from 1973 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 2999.50 EUR Billion in August of 2022 and a record low of 65.40 EUR Billion in Janu

money-supply-m3.desc_snippet: Money Supply M3 in Germany increased to 4145 EUR Billion in March from 4143.40 EUR Billion in February of 2026. Money Supply M3 in Germany averaged 1428.45 EUR Billion from 1969 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 4145.00 EUR Billion in March of 2026 and a record low of 122.90 EUR Billion in Ja

nurses.desc_snippet: Nurses in Germany increased to 14.41 per 1000 people in 2023 from 14.26 per 1000 people in 2022. Nurses in Germany averaged 12.28 per 1000 people from 2000 until 2023, reaching an all time high of 14.41 per 1000 people in 2023 and a record low of 10.18 per 1000 people in 2000.  source: OECD

personal-income-tax-rate.desc_snippet: The Personal Income Tax Rate in Germany stands at 45 percent. Personal Income Tax Rate in Germany averaged 47.39 percent from 1995 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 57.00 percent in 1996 and a record low of 42.00 percent in 2005.  source:  Bundeszentralamt für Steuern

population.desc_snippet: The total population in Germany was estimated at 83.6 million people in 2024, according to the latest census figures and projections from Trading Economics. Looking back, in the year of 1950, Germany had a population of 51.0 million people.  source:  Federal Statistical Office

ppi.desc_snippet: Producer Prices in Germany increased to 126.90 points in March from 123.80 points in February of 2026. Producer Prices in Germany averaged 76.92 points from 1976 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 142.30 points in September of 2022 and a record low of 43.90 points in January of 1976.  source:

productivity.desc_snippet: Productivity in Germany increased to 95.60 points in February from 94.10 points in January of 2026. Productivity in Germany averaged 63.13 points from 1962 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 105.30 points in November of 2017 and a record low of 17.00 points in February of 1962.  source:  Deuts

retail-sales.desc_snippet: Germany’s retail sales dropped 2.0% month-over-month in March 2026, significantly missing market expectations for a milder 0.1% decline and deepening from a downwardly revised 0.3% fall in the previous month. This marked the third consecutive monthly contraction and the sharpest decrease since Octob

retirement-age-men.desc_snippet: Retirement Age Men in Germany increased to 66.33 Years in 2026 from 66.17 Years in 2025. Retirement Age Men in Germany averaged 65.55 Years from 2009 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 66.33 Years in 2026 and a record low of 65.00 Years in 2010.  source:  Bundeszentralamt für Steuern

retirement-age-women.desc_snippet: Retirement Age Women in Germany increased to 66.33 Years in 2026 from 66.17 Years in 2025. Retirement Age Women in Germany averaged 65.55 Years from 2009 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 66.33 Years in 2026 and a record low of 65.00 Years in 2010.  source:  Bundeszentralamt für Steuern

sales-tax-rate.desc_snippet: The Sales Tax Rate in Germany stands at 19 percent. Sales Tax Rate in Germany averaged 18.11 percent from 2000 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 19.00 percent in 2007 and a record low of 16.00 percent in 2001.  source:  Bundeszentralamt für Steuern

services-inflation.desc_snippet: Services Inflation in Germany decreased to 2.8% in April 2026, the lowest since August 2022, from 3.2% in March. Services Inflation in Germany averaged 2.29 percent from 1992 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 9.40 percent in April of 1992 and a record low of 0.30 percent in December of 2010.

social-security-rate.desc_snippet: The Social Security Rate in Germany stands at 38.74 percent. Social Security Rate in Germany averaged 39.78 percent from 1981 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 42.53 percent in 2005 and a record low of 33.35 percent in 1981.  source:  Bundeszentralamt für Steuern

social-security-rate-companies.desc_snippet: The Social Security Rate For Companies in Germany stands at 19.42 percent. Social Security Rate For Companies in Germany averaged 19.75 percent from 1981 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 22.54 percent in 2022 and a record low of 16.70 percent in 1981.  source:  Bundeszentralamt für Steuern

social-security-rate-employees.desc_snippet: The Social Security Rate For Employees in Germany stands at 19.33 percent. Social Security Rate For Employees in Germany averaged 20.03 percent from 1981 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 21.63 percent in 2005 and a record low of 14.12 percent in 2022.  source:  Bundeszentralamt für Steuern

terrorism-index.desc_snippet: Terrorism Index in Germany decreased to 4.45 Points in 2025 from 4.75 Points in 2024. Terrorism Index in Germany averaged 3.48 Points from 2002 until 2025, reaching an all time high of 5.21 Points in 2016 and a record low of 2.10 Points in 2010.  source:  Institute for Economics and Peace

trade-balance.desc_snippet: Germany’s trade surplus narrowed to €14.3 billion in March 2026 from a downwardly revised €19.6 billion in February, falling short of market expectations of €18.4 billion. It was the smallest trade surplus since last November, as exports rose less than imports. Exports unexpectedly increased by 0.5%

unemployed-persons.desc_snippet: The number of unemployed persons in Germany rose by 20,000 from a month earlier to 3.006 million in April 2026, hitting its highest level since March 2011. Unemployed Persons in Germany averaged 2103.57 Thousand from 1950 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 5011.00 Thousand in March of 2005 and

unemployment.desc_snippet: Germany’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate stood at 6.4% in April 2026, unchanged from the previous revised figure and above the expected 6.3%, holding at its highest level since July 2020. The reading highlights the continued effects of three years of economic stagnation on Europe’s largest la

wages.desc_snippet: Wages in Germany increased to 4701 EUR/Month in 2024 from 4479 EUR/Month in 2023. Wages in Germany averaged 3120.09 EUR/Month from 1991 until 2024, reaching an all time high of 4701.00 EUR/Month in 2024 and a record low of 1832.00 EUR/Month in 1991.  source:  Federal Statistical Office

weapons-sales.desc_snippet: Weapons Sales in Germany decreased to 2049 SIPRI TIV Million in 2024 from 2472 SIPRI TIV Million in 2023. Weapons Sales in Germany averaged 1317.64 SIPRI TIV Million from 1953 until 2024, reaching an all time high of 3296.00 SIPRI TIV Million in 2007 and a record low of 12.00 SIPRI TIV Million in 19

withholding-tax-rate.desc_snippet: The Withholding Tax Rate in Germany stands at 0 percent. Withholding Tax Rate in Germany averaged 0.00 percent from 2022 until 2025, reaching an all time high of 0.00 percent in 2023 and a record low of 0.00 percent in 2023.  source:  Bundeszentralamt für Steuern

youth-unemployment-rate.desc_snippet: Youth Unemployment Rate in Germany remained unchanged at 7.50 percent in March. Youth Unemployment Rate in Germany averaged 8.91 percent from 1991 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 16.00 percent in April of 2005 and a record low of 5.70 percent in February of 1991.  source:  Federal Statistic
